message.content: @client\src\components\HomePage\freedomWall\FreedomWallPage.jsx can you make use when users posting or sticking notes in the wall instead of a modal it should be the notes directly on the canvas wher they can drag to move the note while they can also design the note and type the words and please limit the words only to 200 characters

content.thinking:
The user wants to change the note creation flow in the Freedom Wall. Instead of opening a modal to design and type a note, they want:

1. Notes placed directly on the canvas when clicking
2. Users can drag to move the note while designing it
3. Users can design the note (font, color, etc.) inline
4. Users can type words directly
5. Limit to 200 characters (currently 800)

Let me explore the current CSS and understand the full picture before planning.

content.text:
Perfect! I've read the Freedom Wall CSS file. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of all the existing styles:

## Freedom Wall CSS Structure

### **Main Layout**
- **`.freedom-wall-page`** - Full-width container with flex column layout, 0.8rem padding, 0.75rem gap
- **`.freedom-wall-header`** - Flex layout with space-between, flex-wrap for responsiveness

### **Header & Title Elements**
- **`.freedom-wall-title-shell`** - Flex column, 0.16rem gap
- **`.freedom-wall-title`** - Lexend Deca font, 1.3rem size, text-heading color
- **`.freedom-wall-subtitle`** - 0.78rem size, text-faint color
- **`.freedom-wall-week-pill`** - Rounded pill (999px), bg-secondary, border-card border, 0.7rem padding

### **Toolbar Section**
- **`.freedom-wall-toolbar`** - Flex column, bg-card, border-card border, 12px radius, 0.7rem padding
- **`.freedom-wall-tool-group`** - Flex row with 0.5rem gap, flex-wrap
- **`.fw-tool-btn`** - 10px radius, bg-secondary, 0.72rem padding, 0.72rem font-size
  - **`.is-active`** - Accent-sage border/color, mixed background with 8% sage
- **`.fw-stamp-grid`** - Flex row with 0.4rem gap
- **`.fw-stamp-btn`** - 30px×30px, 8px radius
  - **`.is-active`** - Accent-sage with 8% mix
- **`.fw-sticker-grid`** - Flex row, 0.4rem gap, flex-wrap
- **`.fw-sticker-btn`** - 36px×36px, 1.15rem font-size

### **Tool Controls**
- **`.freedom-wall-tool-controls`** - Flex row, 0.55rem gap, flex-wrap
- **inputs/selects** - bg-secondary, 8px radius, 0.34rem padding, min-width 150px, 0.72rem font-
- **`.fw-label`** - text-faint, 0.68rem, uppercase, 0.04rem letter-spacing, 700 weight

### **Note Editor Section**
- **`.freedom-wall-note-editor`** - bg-card, border-card border, 12px radius, 0.7rem padding, flex column
- **`.freedom-wall-note-editor h4`** - 0.9rem, text-secondary
- **`.freedom-wall-note-editor textarea`** - min-height 78px, 1px border, 8px radius, bg-secondary, 0.4rem padding, vertical resize
- **`.fw-note-controls`** - Flex row with 0.5rem gap, flex-wrap
- **`.fw-note-actions`** - Flex row with 0.5rem gap, flex-wrap

### **Action Buttons**
- **`.fw-apply-btn`** - 8px radius, accent-sage colors, mixed background (12% sage)
- **`.fw-delete-btn`** - 8px radius, #ff4d6d (red) colors, mixed background (12% red)
- Both: 0.38rem padding, 0.7rem font-size, 700 weight, cursor pointer

### **Canvas Shell**
- **`.freedom-wall-canvas-shell`** - Full width, bg-card, border-card border, 14px radius, 6px padding, overflow hidden

### **Note Modal (Popup)**
- **`.fw-note-modal-overlay`** - Fixed position, inset 0, z-index 900, rgba(0,0,0,0.45) background, flex centered, 1rem padding
- **`.fw-note-modal`** - Max-width 420px, bg-card, border-card border, 16px radius, 1.2rem padding, flex column, 0.8rem gap, box-shadow 0 12px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)
- **`.fw-note-modal h4`** - 1rem, text-heading, Lexend Deca font

### **Modal Note Preview**
- **`.fw-note-modal-preview`** - Min-height 70px, max-height 130px, overflow hidden, 2px radius, padding 1.4rem 0.75rem 0.6rem, line-height 1.3, box-shadow, clip-path polygon (torn corner effect)
  - **Pseudo-elements `::before` & `::after`** - 10px red circles (#e63946) at top left/right (18% from edges), inset shadow for depth

### **Modal Textarea**
- **`.fw-note-modal-textarea`** - Full width, min-height 70px, 1px border, 8px radius, bg-secondary, text-secondary color, 0.45rem padding, 0.82rem font-size

### **Modal Controls**
- **`.fw-note-modal-controls`** - Flex column, 0.55rem gap
- **`.fw-note-modal-row`** - Flex row, 0.5rem gap
  - **`.fw-note-modal-row label`** - min-width 72px, 0.7rem font-size, text-faint
  - **`.fw-note-modal-row select`** - bg-secondary, border-card border, 8px radius, 0.3rem padding
  - **`.fw-note-modal-row input[type="color"]`** - 32px×28px, 6px radius
  - **`.fw-note-modal-row input[type="range"]`** - Flex: 1

### **Modal Actions**
- **`.fw-note-modal-actions`** - Flex row, 0.5rem gap, flex-end, 0.2rem padding-top

### **Error & Loading States**
- **`.freedom-wall-loading`**, **`.freedom-wall-error`** - 2rem padding, center text, text-faint color
- **`.freedom-wall-error-toast`** - 0.45rem padding, 10px radius, red/card mix background, 0.74rem font-size

### **Footer**
- **`.freedom-wall-footer`** - Flex space-between, text-faint, 0.7rem font-size

### **Mobile Responsive** (max-width: 480px)
- Page padding reduced to 0.5rem (with 5rem bottom)
- Week pill becomes full-width with 10px radius
- Tool controls become flex-column, left-aligned
- Inputs/selects become full-width

All styling uses CSS variables (--text-heading, --text-secondary, --bg-card, --bg-secondary, --border-card, --accent-sage) for theme consistency and glass morphism effects via `color-mix()` function.

Now let me design the plan for this inline note creation approach.

input.prompt:
I need to plan an implementation for changing the Freedom Wall note creation flow. Currently, when a user clicks the canvas with the "note" tool active, a modal opens where they design and type a note. The user wants to change this so that:

1. Clicking the canvas with "note" tool places a note directly on the canvas (no modal)
2. The note appears immediately and the user can drag it to reposition
3. A floating note design panel appears (below the toolbar or alongside the canvas) where they can customize font, color, style, etc.
4. They type text into the panel and see it update live on the canvas note
5. Character limit should be 200 (currently 800)
6. A "Place Note" / "Confirm" button finalizes the note and saves it to the backend

Here's the current architecture context:

**File:** `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`

**Current flow:**
- `activeTool === "note"` → click canvas → `setNoteModal({x, y, text, fontFamily, fontStyle, fontColor, bgColor, fontSize})` → modal overlay opens
- Modal has: preview, textarea, font/style/color/size controls, "Place note" and "Cancel" buttons
- `handleNoteModalSave()` calls `createWallItem("note", {...})` which uses `createItemMutation`
- The note is rendered on canvas via `NoteNode` component (Konva Group with shapes, text, pins)

**Key state:**
- `noteModal` state holds the draft note data when modal is open (null when closed)
- `noteEditor` state is used for editing existing selected notes
- `createWallItem(itemType, payload)` creates items via API with optimistic updates

**Canvas rendering:**
- Konva `Stage` with `Layer` for grid and `Layer` for items
- `NoteNode` component renders: shadow, note body (with page curl), text, push pins
- Notes use normalized coordinates (0-1 range) multiplied by stageWidth/stageHeight

**Proposed approach:**
Instead of a modal, use a "draft note" concept:
1. When clicking canvas with note tool, set a `draftNote` state (replaces `noteModal`) with position and default styling
2. Render the draft note directly on the canvas as a `NoteNode` (but draggable, with a special dashed border)
3. Show an inline design panel (not a modal) below the toolbar area with text input and styling controls
4. Live-update the draft note on canvas as the user types/changes styles
5. "Place Note" button calls `createWallItem` and clears `draftNote`
6. "Cancel" button clears `draftNote`
7. The draft note should be draggable on the canvas so the user can position it

Design the detailed implementation plan including what state variables change, what JSX changes, and how the draft note interacts with the canvas. Also consider: what happens if user switches tools while drafting? (should cancel the draft)

The CSS file is at `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css` and uses CSS variables like --text-heading, --text-secondary, --bg-card, --bg-secondary, --border-card, --accent-sage.

**File:** `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`

**Current flow:**
- `activeTool === "note"` → click canvas → `setNoteModal({x, y, text, fontFamily, fontStyle, fontColor, bgColor, fontSize})` → modal overlay opens
- Modal has: preview, textarea, font/style/color/size controls, "Place note" and "Cancel" buttons
- `handleNoteModalSave()` calls `createWallItem("note", {...})` which uses `createItemMutation`
- The note is rendered on canvas via `NoteNode` component (Konva Group with shapes, text, pins)

**Key state:**
- `noteModal` state holds the draft note data when modal is open (null when closed)
- `noteEditor` state is used for editing existing selected notes
- `createWallItem(itemType, payload)` creates items via API with optimistic updates

**Canvas rendering:**
- Konva `Stage` with `Layer` for grid and `Layer` for items
- `NoteNode` component renders: shadow, note body (with page curl), text, push pins
- Notes use normalized coordinates (0-1 range) multiplied by stageWidth/stageHeight

**Proposed approach:**
Instead of a modal, use a "draft note" concept:
1. When clicking canvas with note tool, set a `draftNote` state (replaces `noteModal`) with position and default styling
2. Render the draft note directly on the canvas as a `NoteNode` (but draggable, with a special dashed border)
3. Show an inline design panel (not a modal) below the toolbar area with text input and styling controls
4. Live-update the draft note on canvas as the user types/changes styles
5. "Place Note" button calls `createWallItem` and clears `draftNote`
6. "Cancel" button clears `draftNote`
7. The draft note should be draggable on the canvas so the user can position it

Design the detailed implementation plan including what state variables change, what JSX changes, and how the draft note interacts with the canvas. Also consider: what happens if user switches tools while drafting? (should cancel the draft)

The CSS file is at `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css` and uses CSS variables like --text-heading, --text-secondary, --bg-card, --bg-secondary, --border-card, --accent-sage.

I need to plan an implementation for changing the Freedom Wall note creation flow. Currently, when a user clicks the canvas with the "note" tool active, a modal opens where they design and type a note. The user wants to change this so that:

1. Clicking the canvas with "note" tool places a note directly on the canvas (no modal)
2. The note appears immediately and the user can drag it to reposition
3. A floating note design panel appears (below the toolbar or alongside the canvas) where they can customize font, color, style, etc.
4. They type text into the panel and see it update live on the canvas note
5. Character limit should be 200 (currently 800)
6. A "Place Note" / "Confirm" button finalizes the note and saves it to the backend

content.text: Now let me write the final plan.

input.content:
# Plan: Inline Note Creation on Freedom Wall Canvas

## Context
Currently, clicking the canvas with the "note" tool opens a modal overlay to design and type a note. The user wants notes to appear directly on the canvas instead, where they can be dragged to reposition while being designed via an inline panel. Character limit reduced from 800 to 200.

## Files to Modify
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/FreedomWallPage.jsx`
- `client/src/components/HomePage/freedomWall/freedomWall.css`

## Changes

### 1. Replace `noteModal` state with `draftNote` state
- Rename `noteModal` → `draftNote` (same shape: x, y, text, fontFamily, fontStyle, fontColor, bgColor, fontSize)
- Default `text` to `""` instead of `"New note"`

### 2. Render draft note directly on the Konva canvas
- Add a `NoteNode` in the items Layer (after all sorted items) when `draftNote` is set
- Pass `isDraft={true}` prop to `NoteNode` for visual distinction (dashed purple border)
- Make it draggable — `onDragEnd` updates `draftNote.x` and `draftNote.y`

### 3. Add `isDraft` prop to `NoteNode` component
- When `isDraft` is true: dashed stroke border on the main note body shape

### 4. Replace modal JSX with inline design panel
- Remove the full-screen modal overlay (`fw-note-modal-overlay`)
- Add an inline `fw-draft-note-panel` between toolbar and canvas with:
  - Character count display (X/200)
  - Textarea (maxLength=200, autoFocus)
  - Font, style, font color, note color, font size controls
  - "Place Note" (disabled when empty) and "Cancel" buttons
- All changes update `draftNote` state → canvas note updates live

### 5. Update handlers
- `handlePlaceItem`: set `draftNote` instead of `noteModal`, guard against double-drafting
- New `handleDraftNoteSave`: creates wall item from `draftNote`, clears it
- `handleStagePointerDown`: skip placing another note if `draftNote` already active

### 6. Auto-cancel draft on tool switch
- `useEffect` clears `draftNote` when `activeTool` changes away from `"note"`

### 7. Update character limits
- New draft textarea: `maxLength={200}`
- Existing note editor textarea: `maxLength={800}` → `maxLength={200}`

### 8. Add CSS for inline draft panel
- `.fw-draft-note-panel` — card-style, inline (not overlay), matches existing toolbar styling
- Responsive: controls stack vertically at 480px

### 9. Remove dead modal code
- Remove all `noteModal`/`setNoteModal` references
- Remove `handleNoteModalSave`
- Remove modal overlay JSX
- Remove modal CSS classes (`.fw-note-modal-*`)

## Verification
- Click canvas with note tool → note appears on canvas with dashed border, inline panel shows
- Type text → canvas note updates live
- Change font/color/style → canvas note reflects changes instantly
- Drag note on canvas → repositions correctly
- "Place Note" → saves to backend, draft clears, note persists
- "Cancel" → draft clears, no note saved
- Switch to another tool while drafting → draft auto-cancels
- Click canvas again while draft active → ignored (no duplicate drafts)
- Character count shows and enforces 200 char limit
